DataRequest.techniqueName: Network Denial of Service: Direct Network Flood
DataRequest.Name: Denial of Service attack detected by policy old
DataRequest.Description:
A Denial of Service (DoS) policy examines network traffic arriving at a FortiGate interface for anomalous patterns, which usually indicates an attack.
A denial of service occurs when an attacking system starts an abnormally large number of sessions with a target system. The large number of sessions slows down or disables the target system, preventing legitimate users from using it.
DoS policies are checked before security policies, preventing attacks from triggering more resource intensive security protection and slowing down the FortiGate.
https://docs.fortinet.com/document/fortigate/6.2.16/cookbook/771644/dos-protection
DataRequest.IncidentTitle: Network Denial of Service policy was targeting $destIpAddr

SubPattern.name: DoS_Policy_events
SubPattern.SingleEvtConstr: (attackName="tcp_syn_flood" OR attackName="tcp_src_session" OR attackName="tcp_dst_session" OR attackName="udp_flood" OR attackName="udp_scan" OR attackName="udp_src_session" OR attackName="udp_dst_session" OR attackName="icmp_flood" OR attackName="icmp_sweep" OR attackName="icmp_src_session" OR attackName="icmp_dst_session" OR attackName="ip_src_session" OR attackName="ip_dst_session" OR attackName="sctp_flood" OR attackName="sctp_scan" OR attackName="sctp_src_session" OR attackName="sctp_dst_session") AND eventType CONTAIN "FortiGate"
